claim: Context constraints act as soft constraints on models, similar to guardrails; using curated docs instead of internet produces better results
actionable: Replace open internet access with high-quality, domain-specific documentation to improve agent reasoning and reduce noise.
source_title: Bounded Autonomy: Between Free Will and Determinism

claim: Models are naturally verbose and tend toward complexity; simpler solutions (e.g., HTML over agent apps) often perform 10-100x better
actionable: Before building agentic solutions, test simple alternatives; if direct HTML/SQL/scripting solves the problem, use that instead.
source_title: Bounded Autonomy: Between Free Will and Determinism

claim: Constraints drive creativity; smaller context windows and limited capabilities force more clever solutions than unlimited resources
actionable: Intentionally constrain agent capabilities and context windows to encourage more efficient, focused solutions.
source_title: Bounded Autonomy: Between Free Will and Determinism

claim: AI is fundamentally a translation process between representation spaces; same content can be structured as markdown, graphs, clusters, or folders
actionable: Design systems with multiple representation structures (markdown, graphs, clustering, folders) to enable flexible data transformation.
source_title: Bounded Autonomy: Between Free Will and Determinism

claim: Larger context windows don't solve fundamental problems; global knowledge doubles every 12 hours so even 1M tokens will be insufficient
actionable: Don't rely on context window growth to solve information problems; implement selection/prioritization mechanisms to curate what goes in context.
source_title: Bounded Autonomy: Between Free Will and Determinism

claim: Long-running agents require explicit context organization (history, action logs, goals, plans) to avoid forgetting mid-task
actionable: For multi-step agents, store structured trace logs including: executed actions, tool outputs, current goal state, and plan modifications.
source_title: Bounded Autonomy: Between Free Will and Determinism

claim: Don't automate a task unless you can do it yourself first; understanding manual workflow is prerequisite for agent design
actionable: Before automating with agents, manually execute the workflow once; identify decision points, edge cases, and success criteria.
source_title: Bounded Autonomy: Between Free Will and Determinism
